data.title: Berkshire 13F: NYT In, Apple and Amazon Cut — What It Means for Investors
data.description:
Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B) recently filed its 13F for Q4 2025 — and the changes are catching investors' attention. The company disclosed a fresh investment in The New York Times (NYT) while trimming positions in tech heavyweights Apple (AAPL) and Amazon (AMZN). The filing suggests a more selective strategy. Rather than just riding the wave in mega-cap tech and AI names, Berkshire appears to be rebalancing its portfolio — adding a media stock for the first time since 2020 while trimming exposure to certain tech holdings.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

By trimming positions in companies like Apple and Amazon, the firm is showing discipline rather than chasing momentum at any price. For investors, the message is simple. Even long-term tech believers adjust their holdings when the market changes. Smart investing isn't about chasing hype or jumping into every rally. It's about staying disciplined, controlling risk, and setting up your portfolio for the next phase of the market.

Notably, a 13F filing is a list of stock holdings that large investment managers are required to disclose to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) every quarter.

Berkshire Makes Big Bet on NYT

In Q4 2025, Berkshire bought 5.1 million shares of NYT, worth about $351.7 million. The Times, once known mainly as a traditional newspaper, has successfully shifted to digital. It now has nearly 13 million subscribers. This move brings Berkshire back into the newspaper business for the first time since 2020, when it sold its media holdings, including the Omaha World-Herald. Even so, the NYT investment is very small compared to Berkshire's massive portfolio. It makes up just 0.13% of its public stock holdings.

In addition, the company increased its stakes in Chubb (CB), Chevron (CVX), Domino's Pizza (DPZ), and Lamar Advertising (LAMR) in the fourth quarter. The additions suggest areas it has historically favored for steady cash flow and long-term value.

Berkshire Shifts AI Strategy

The company slashed its stake in Amazon by 77%, reducing it from 0.82% of the portfolio to just 0.02%. Berkshire had already been trimming the position for several quarters, and the sharp year-end cut suggests a step-by-step de-risking — not a sudden shift in view. The move also comes as Amazon ramps up spending, with plans for roughly $200 billion in capital expenditures in 2026.

At the same time, Berkshire reduced its stake in Apple by about 4%. Even after the trim, Apple remains its largest holding at 22.6% of the portfolio. That reflects Berkshire's preference for steady, visible cash flows — along with Apple's AI push without the heavy data center spending that cloud giants face.

data.title: Meta Platforms (META) to Spend $65 Million Pushing 'AI Agenda' During U.S. Midterms
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) is planning to spend $65 million this year to push its "AI agenda" during the U.S. midterm election cycle.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The New York Times (NYT) is reporting that Meta is planning to support state politicians who are friendly to the artificial intelligence (AI) industry, starting with politicians based in Texas and Illinois. This year's political spending is the biggest ever from Meta Platforms, which owns social media sites Facebook and Instagram.

The millions being spent during this year's midterms is a reversal for Meta, which previously made only small donations to political action committees and contributed to presidential inaugurations after elections had occurred. But now, Meta Platforms is getting more politically active as it worries about regulatory threats to the AI sector.

Meta's Super PACs

In addition to spending $65 million to support pro-AI candidates and lobby for supportive legislation, Meta Platforms is also starting two new super PACs. One group, Forge the Future Project, is backing Republicans. Another, Making Our Tomorrow, is getting behind Democratic candidates who are running for seats in the U.S. Congress.

The new Super PACs join two others that Meta has already launched, one focused on California and the other an umbrella organization. Meta's political activities are set to begin in Texas, where the company has three AI data center projects, and where politicians are debating imposing a moratorium on the construction of new data centers.

Is META Stock a Buy?

The stock of Meta Platforms has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 43 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 39 Buy and four Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average META price target of $852.42 implies 34% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Why Are Stock Market Futures Steady Today, 2/18/26?
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were steady Wednesday night after the major indexes ended the day higher. Traders also looked ahead to Walmart's (WMT) quarterly results, due Thursday morning. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index inched up 0.07%, 0.02%, and 0.03%, respectively, at 9:15 p.m. EST on February 18.

During Wednesday's regular session, the S&P 500, the Nasdaq, and the Dow Jones climbed 0.6%, 0.8%, and 0.3%, respectively. Tech leaders in the "Magnificent Seven," along with financial and energy stocks, helped lift the market.

Geopolitical tensions on Wednesday pushed oil prices up more than 4% after Vice President JD Vance said Iran failed to meet key U.S. demands in this week's nuclear talks. He added that President Donald Trump could still use military force if diplomacy does not stop Iran's nuclear program.

Investors also inflation-remains-above-2-minutes-show">reviewed minutes from the Federal Reserve's January meeting, which showed officials were divided on the interest-rate outlook.

Walmart's earnings on Thursday will be closely watched, given the retailer's strong performance in 2026 so far and its recent move into the $1 trillion market cap club. Also, traders are awaiting weekly jobless claims and pending home sales data due tomorrow.

data.title: THNQ and ROBO: Two AI ETFs Worth Considering Despite Bubble Concerns
data.description:
Artificial intelligence (AI) stocks have soared over the past year, and with valuations hitting new highs, fears of an "AI bubble" are growing. Despite these concerns, demand for AI remains strong in nearly every corner of the economy, from cloud computing and semiconductors to cybersecurity and enterprise software.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For investors who want long-term AI exposure without betting on a single high-flying stock, ETFs offer an easier, more balanced way to stay invested while managing risk. ROBO Global Artificial Intelligence ETF (THNQ) and ROBO Global Robotics & Automation Index ETF (ROBO) are two AI-focused ETFs that still look appealing, even as bubble concerns rise.

1. ROBO Global Artificial Intelligence ETF (THNQ)

THNQ ETF provides exposure to companies building and using AI, from machine learning and cloud software to automation and computer vision. It offers a diversified way to invest in AI without relying on just a few big tech names.

Some of the top holdings in the THNQ ETF include Lumentum Holdings (LITE), Palo Alto Networks (PANW), and Analog Devices (ADI). Overall, the ETF has $286.73 million in assets under management (AUM) and an expense ratio of 0.68%. Over the past six months, the THNQ ETF has generated a return of 12.28%.

On TipRanks, THNQ has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 43 Buys and five Holds assigned in the last three months. At $81.08, the average THNQ ETF price target implies 26.47% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Slips on New Concerns Over Nvidia/Meta Partnership
data.description:
Chip stock Intel (INTC) may have a new problem to watch out for going forward, as one of its biggest supporters, Nvidia (NVDA) seems to be hedging its bets. Nvidia recently stepped up its partnership with Meta Platforms (META), and that combination could pose problems for Intel going forward. Investors seemed concerned as well, and Intel shares were down nearly 1.5% in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nvidia plans to put more of its Grace central processing units (CPUs) into place at Meta data centers. Meta is about to launch what reports call "…the first large-scale Nvidia Grace-only deployment." That alone might not mean so much, but some are concerned that this is a sign that Intel may be about to face a lot harder competition in the data center space, a point that Intel was clearly leading previously.

Worse for Intel, Nvidia has a better competitive proposition than merely "at least we're not Intel." Nvidia reports that its chips offer better performance per watt for the data center. Given the increasing difficulty of finding watts to run data centers these days, more power-efficient chips are worth considering. In Meta's case, they were worth going with over Intel.

Many Happy Returns

This brings us to the issue of a recent Turkish wedding. I know, that is one seriously strange segue, but stick with me. A normal Turkish wedding often involves high-end gifts for the bride and groom, normally, gold and jewelry. But in this particular wedding, the bride and groom landed an array of computer hardware instead.

The groom, at one point, was caught sporting an MSI Suprim GeForce RTX 5090 GPU, which was hung around his neck with a length of red ribbon. The bride picked up a quad-channel DDR5 memory kit, again adorned in red ribbon. Apparently, the value of these things in the Turkish market is actually exceeding gold, so in keeping with Turkish wedding tradition, the bride and groom landed the best of computer hardware.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on eight Buys, 21 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 79.55% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $48.21 per share implies 6.05% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Gains, California Sales No Longer Threatened
data.description:
For a while there, electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) was on the verge of not being allowed to sell cars in California. Most of this was due to marketing efforts involving its Autopilot and Full Self-Driving (FSD) features. But new reports note that the risk has passed, and Tesla has cleaned up its marketing to California's apparent satisfaction. The news was good enough for investors, who gave Tesla shares nearly an extra 1.5% boost in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Late last year, an administrative judge in California found that Tesla misled drivers about just how well a Tesla car can drive itself. The terms "Autopilot" and "Full Self-Driving" in particular rankled this judge, who declared the claims misleading and recommended Tesla's license to sell be suspended for 30 days.

Regulators—potentially cognizant of the increasing difficulties involved with getting gas in the state—gave Tesla a 90-day window to make changes to its advertising. Tesla removed the term "Autopilot" altogether, and now includes the term "supervised" when talking about FSD.

That Model Y May Have Depreciated, A Lot

Meanwhile, new reports emerged to suggest that the Model Y might not have held onto its value very well. Back in 2021, demand for a Model Y was brisk, to the point where a used one was actually more expensive than a brand new one. Those days are long since gone. One report notes that the Model Y lost over 60% of its value in just five years.

The reports do vary, however. A Kelley Blue Book report said that the Model Y went from around $40,000 to about $21,000. That is a loss of just under 50% of value, not the 60% suggested earlier. Still, it is a significant loss, particularly when compared with the initial outlay. Plus, for used shoppers, a Tesla may be more affordable than many other options.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 13.89% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $396.80 per share implies 4.64% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Rally as Fed Eyes Rate Hikes to Counter Inflation; Industrial Sector Posts Solid Growth
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed Wednesday in positive territory, although a warning from the Fed put investors on high alert.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Instead of rate cuts, higher interest rates could be on the table if inflation remains above 2%, minutes from January's Fed meeting showed. Inflation remains a key concern for many officials, with several considering the "possibility that upward adjustments to the target range for the federal funds rate could be appropriate if inflation remains at above-target levels."

The minutes also noted that a majority of officials believe the risk of persistent inflation is still present, while downside risks to employment have softened in recent months. Lower rates can benefit the labor market but risk pushing inflation higher through increased borrowing and consumer spending.

The Fed will announce its next interest rate decision on March 18, with 94.1% odds of rates staying unchanged on the CME FedWatch tool. By year-end, the most likely scenario is for the central bank to cut rates by 50 bps to 3.00%-3.25%.

Meanwhile, the Trump administration's goal of increasing domestic production is gaining ground. In January, U.S. industrial production rose by 0.7% from the prior month, marking the highest rate of growth since February 2025. Several factors may have contributed to the growth, including easing trade uncertainty and favorable tax policies. Efficiency is also on the rise, with factory capacity utilization reaching 75.6% during the month, the highest since September and improving from 75.2% in December.

Elsewhere, the S&P 500 (SPX) is having its worst start to the year compared to global peers since 1995. The benchmark index is essentially flat year-to-date, while the iShares MSCI ACWI ex U.S. ETF (ACWX), which tracks global markets excluding the U.S., has returned 7.4%. Geopolitical tensions and volatile economic policies, including tariffs, President Trump's threat to annex Greenland, and concerns about overspending in the AI industry, have pushed many investors to shift their capital away from the U.S.

U.S. tensions with Iran have also unsettled some investors. While both sides touted progress in nuclear talks on Tuesday, Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei continues to send out aggressive statements on X, including a post that warned the U.S. military "may at times be struck so hard that it cannot get up again."

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.56% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 0.80%.

data.title: Spotify Stock (SPOT) Crashes After Google Unveils New AI Music Feature
data.description:
Shares of music streaming company Spotify (SPOT) fell 5% immediately after Google parent company Alphabet (GOOGL) unveiled a new artificial intelligence (AI) music feature.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Specifically, Alphabet announced that its Gemini AI model can now generate music and lyrics for users, raising questions about potential impacts for Spotify, which, until now, has had a strong hold on music streaming around the world.

Alphabet's new Lyria 3 music generation model, released in beta to Gemini app users, allows people to create 30-second tracks using text prompts and images. The feature can also generate vocal and instrumental compositions across various music genres.

Music for the Masses

Users of Gemini can also create music by describing their ideas in text or by uploading photos that the system analyzes to generate matching tracks. Each creation includes custom cover art made by Google's Nano Banana image generation system.

Alphabet said that it is also integrating Lyria 3 into its YouTube's Dream Track feature for content creators. YouTube, also owned by Alphabet, is another popular place for users to access music content. Analysts say the immediate reaction to SPOT stock reflects investor worries about AI's impacts on established music platforms and streaming services.

Is GOOGL Stock a Buy?

The stock of Alphabet has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 32 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 25 Buy and seven Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average GOOGL price target of $379.47 implies 25.01% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Has the Market Misjudged Palantir? Mizuho Upgrades PLTR Stock
data.description:
For much of the past year, investors have questioned whether Palantir (PLTR) is a real AI business or simply a hype-driven trade. Year-to-date, the stock has declined 24%. After the stock pulled back at the start of 2026, Mizuho analyst Gregg Moskowitz said the drop has actually created an opportunity. The analyst upgraded the shares to Outperform from Neutral and kept a $195 price target, implying about 47% upside from current levels.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The main worry was not growth but valuation. The stock surged during the AI rally and investors feared a sharp re-rating. Moskowitz said that reset has already happened. Palantir's valuation multiple fell about 46% in the first six weeks of the year even as the business improved, making the risk-reward "newly attractive."

What Is the Real Catalyst for the Upgrade?

The biggest driver behind the upgrade is Palantir's commercial business. U.S. commercial revenue grew 137% year-over-year in the last quarter, suggesting companies are moving beyond AI experiments and into real deployment.

The analyst believes enterprises are embedding Palantir's AIP software directly into daily operations, which makes the platform harder to replace and supports long-term growth.

The government segment also remains strong. U.S. government revenue grew 66% in the latest quarter and represents about 41% of total revenue. With ongoing defense modernization and geopolitical tensions, Moskowitz expects public-sector demand to stay durable.

Why the Analyst Thinks Palantir Is Different

Some investors worry Palantir does not build its own AI models, but Mizuho said that misses the point. The company's value lies in integrating data, security, and workflows so organizations can deploy AI safely inside real systems rather than simply accessing a model.

As AI models become more widely available, the analyst believes that operational layer could become even more important.

Is PLTR Stock a Buy? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on PLTR stock based on 12 Buys, five Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average PLTR price target of $191.25 per share implies 43.78% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
